faculty: Giurisprudenza (Law), Department of Legal and Economic Studies
admission_type: restricted access – verification of requirements and personal preparation
admission_test_type: Verification of curricular requirements and personal preparation; no standardized written entrance test
application_platform: MoveIN platform for non-EU pre-selection; Infostud for admission

academic_requirements.prior_degree: A three-year bachelor's degree (Laurea or equivalent) in Law, Economics, Political Science, International Relations, or related field

language_requirements.level: B2 CEFR in English (general Sapienza requirement; programme taught entirely in English)
language_requirements.note: Programme is entirely taught in English, targeting international students and Italians wishing to acquire a strong command of EU law and economics.

italian_language_requirement: Italian not required for admission; programme taught entirely in English.
application_deadline_eu: 31 July 2026 for EU and EU-equivalent candidates [uncertain]
application_deadline_non_eu: MoveIN pre-selection window 22 December 2025 – 15 May 2026 for non-EU candidates residing abroad
application_fee: €30 per application via MoveIN (max 2 per candidate)
required_documents: Bachelor's degree certificate or enrolment certificate
For foreign qualifications: Declaration of Value (DoV) or CIMEA statement
document_translation_authentication: Foreign documents require official translation and legalization/apostille plus Declaration of Value (DoV) or CIMEA statement.

entry_exam_or_test.selection: Verification of curricular requirements and personal preparation

source_quotes.quote: Programme: European studies; Degree Class: LM-90; Course Code: 33468; Faculty: Giurisprudenza; Language: English
Entirely taught in English. EU's Law and Economics curriculum: Economics of European Integration, European and Italian Public Law, Environmental Law and Economics.

uncertainty_notes: PROGRAMME NAME CORRECTION: Original outline item 'European Economy and Business Law' does not exist as a Sapienza programme. The correct name is 'European Studies' (Studi europei), code 33468, LM-90. Programme updated accordingly.
This programme covers EU law and economics, international relations — it does NOT have 'Business Law' in its title, but overlaps significantly in content.
Three curricula: EU's Law and Economics, International Relations, and Development Cooperation (confirm on programme page).
Specific curricular ECTS requirements not extracted.
Per-programme non-EU quota and places not extracted.
